title: Helleborine tenii
intro: Helleborine tenii, also known as Epipactis tenii, is a species of orchid native to China, characterized by its delicate white flowers and slender leaves. Traditionally, Helleborine tenii has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, Helleborine tenii is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create herbal remedies for respiratory and cardiovascular health.
taxonomy: Helleborine tenii is a type of orchid, specifically classified within the genus Helleborine. This genus is a part of the subtribe Orchidinae and the tribe Orchideae. Helleborine tenii is further classified as a species of epiphytic orchid, which grows on other plants for support. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae and is native to Asia. The exact classification may vary slightly depending on the source, but it is generally agreed upon to be an epiphytic orchid species.
